title: daucus azoricus
intro: Daucus azoricus, also known as the Azores carrot or Carrot of the Azores, is a plant species in the carrot family, native to the Azores Islands in Portugal. It is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly as a digestive aid and to reduce inflammation, thanks to its rich content of sesquiterpenes and other bioactive compounds. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome, improving digestion, and alleviating pain. Daucus azoricus can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
